content:
🚀 claude-usage | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a local, detailed dashboard for monitoring and analyzing their Claude Code token usage and associated costs.
✨ Key Features: • Tracks token usage (input, output, cache) • Logs session history, models, and projects • Estimates costs based on usage • Provides a web-based dashboard with charts • Works with API, Pro, and Max plans
📖 Summary: This Python repository provides a local dashboard to visualize and track your Claude Code token usage, session history, and estimated costs. It works by parsing local log files generated by Claude Code, offering insights beyond what the official UI provides, and is compatible with API, Pro, and Max plans.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 metrics | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To create and display personalized, visually appealing statistics about a GitHub user's activity and contributions directly within their profile README or other web content.
✨ Key Features: • Infographics generator for GitHub statistics • 30+ plugins and 335+ options for customization • Supports users, organizations, and repositories • Renders metrics as SVG, Markdown, PDF, or JSON • Embeddable in GitHub profile readmes
📖 Summary: Metrics is a JavaScript-based tool that generates customizable infographics displaying GitHub account statistics. It offers over 30 plugins and 300 options to visualize data such as contributions, languages, and stargazers, rendering them as SVG, Markdown, PDF, or JSON.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 open-wearables | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified API and self-hosted platform for developers and individuals to aggregate, manage, and analyze health data from diverse wearable devices, enabling faster development of health applications and personalized health insights.
✨ Key Features: • Unified API for multiple wearable providers
📖 Summary: Open Wearables is an open-source, self-hosted platform designed to unify health data from various wearable devices into a single, AI-ready API. It simplifies the process for developers and individuals to access, manage, and derive insights from their health metrics, offering a unified interface instead of managing multiple device-specific integrations.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 nano11 | PowerShell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating a minimal, static Windows 11 environment for testing, development, or embedded use in virtual machines.
✨ Key Features: • Automates the build of a streamlined Windows 11 image using PowerShell. • Utilizes only built-in DISM capabilities and oscdimg.exe. • Creates a bootable ISO with no external binaries. • Includes an unattended answer file to bypass Microsoft Account requirement and enable compact installation.
📖 Summary: nano11 is a PowerShell script that automates the creation of a highly streamlined Windows 11 ISO image. It aggressively removes non-essential components, apps, and services to achieve a significantly smaller footprint, making it suitable for testing, development, or embedded use cases in virtual machines.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 browserytools | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a wide array of free, privacy-focused, client-side tools for everyday productivity tasks and developer utilities directly within the web browser.
✨ Key Features: • 136+ free browser-based tools • 100% client-side operation (no servers, no uploads, full privacy)
📖 Summary: BrowseryTools is a comprehensive suite of over 136 free, browser-based tools designed for productivity and developers. All tools operate entirely client-side, ensuring user privacy and eliminating the need for server uploads. The collection includes image manipulation, file conversion, code formatting, AI-powered on-device utilities, and more.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
